rationale: The cited paper is a substantive marketing study on how online reviews affect sales and uses a differences-in-differences style approach, and the citing paper also relies heavily on DID estimation for spending outcomes. However, the evidence provided is only a DOI-level reference match with no in-text citation context, and the citing note does not explicitly mention word-of-mouth or reviews. With this sparse/mixed evidence, it is not safe to drop as obvious mention-only.
